(2) ~~Closure-held `measure_cache` survives `graph.destroy()`~~ — **resolved 2026-04-03:** `on_message` now clears `measure_cache` and calls `clear_cache()` on both INVALIDATE and TEARDOWN. (3) `SvgBoundsAdapter` regex may match nested `<svg>` elements or content inside XML comments/CDATA — resolved: strip `<!--...-->` and `<![CDATA[...]]>` before viewBox extraction; document single-root-SVG constraint; expose `SvgParser` protocol so users can opt in their own parser. (4) ~~`ImageSizeAdapter` returns mutable references~~ — **resolved 2026-04-03:** `measure_image` now returns `dict(dims)` (shallow copy).

---

title: Runner protocol and async utilities
body:
- **No ThreadRunner default (pre-1.0):** Python removes `ThreadRunner` (daemon-thread-per-coroutine). Users must explicitly call `set_default_runner(AsyncioRunner.from_running())` or `set_default_runner(TrioRunner(nursery))`. TS has no equivalent concern (native async). If graphrefly-ts adds a non-browser runtime (e.g. Deno, Bun workers), a similar explicit runner setup should be required.
- **`call_soon_threadsafe` always (no loop-thread detection):** Async utilities always use `loop.call_soon_threadsafe()` for cross-boundary scheduling. The earlier `_is_loop_thread` optimization was removed because first-call heuristic was fundamentally broken (cached the wrong thread id if first called from a non-loop thread). If perf profiling shows `call_soon_threadsafe` as a bottleneck, revisit with `asyncio.get_running_loop() == loop` (try/except for non-async callers).
- **`to_async_iter` yields on both DATA and RESOLVED:** Yields the current value on `RESOLVED` (no-change cycles) in addition to `DATA` values. This ensures repeated identical values (e.g. `of(1, 2, 2)` feeding through a derived) are all yielded. TS should match if it adds an equivalent utility.
- **Cancel race prevention:** `AsyncioRunner` and `TrioRunner` use a `cancelled` flag checked inside `_create_task`/`_wrapper` to handle cancellation before the task/scope is created. TS equivalent should guard similarly.
- **Structured concurrency preservation:** Runner wrappers re-raise `CancelledError` (asyncio) and `Cancelled` (trio) instead of routing them to `on_error`. This preserves structured-concurrency contracts. TS should re-throw `AbortError` / framework-specific cancellation signals similarly.

title: AI surface behavioral semantics parity
body:
Cross-language notes for `patterns.ai` / `graphrefly.patterns.ai`. **Keep this subsection aligned in both repos’ `docs/optimizations.md`.**

| Topic | Resolution |
|-------|------------|
| **`agent_loop` / `agentLoop` — LLM adapter output** | `invoke` may return a plain `LLMResponse`, or any `NodeInput` (including `Node`, awaitables, async iterables). Implementations coerce with `fromAny` / `from_any`, prefer a synchronous `get()` when it already holds an `LLMResponse`, then **block until the first settled `DATA`** (`subscribe` + `Promise` in TypeScript; `first_value_from` in Python). Do not unsubscribe immediately after `subscribe` without waiting for emissions. |
| **`toolRegistry` / `tool_registry` — handler output** | Handlers may return plain values, Promise-like values, or reactive `NodeInput`. **TypeScript:** `execute` awaits Promise-likes, then resolves **only** `Node` / `AsyncIterable` via `fromAny` + first `DATA` (do **not** pass arbitrary strings through `fromAny` — it treats strings as iterables and emits per character). **Python:** `execute` uses `from_any` + `first_value_from` only for awaitables, async iterables, or `Node`; plain values return as-is. |
| **~~`agentMemory` / `agent_memory` — factory scope (implemented, verified 2026-04-07)~~** | Fully wired in both TS and PY. All five primitives (`knowledgeGraph`/`knowledge_graph`, `vectorIndex`/`vector_index`, `lightCollection`/`light_collection`, `decay`, `autoCheckpoint`/`auto_checkpoint`) are opt-in via options. Retrieval pipeline (4-stage: vector search + KG expansion + scoring + budget packing) and periodic reflection also wired. |

title: _invoke_llm defensive alignment
body: | **3** | **~~`_invoke_llm` / `_invokeLLM` defensive alignment (resolved 2026-04-07 — already implemented)~~** | Both ports reject `None`/`null`, reject plain `str`, normalize `dict` with `content` key to `LLMResponse`, and route remaining shapes through `from_any` + `first_value_from`. Aligned. |

title: LLMInvokeOptions + cooperative cancellation
body: | **4** | **~~`LLMInvokeOptions` + cooperative cancellation (implemented 2026-04-07)~~** | Both ports now wire cancellation from the agent loop into `adapter.invoke()`. **TS:** `AbortSignal` via `LLMInvokeOptions.signal`. **PY:** `CancellationToken` protocol (`core/cancellation.py`) with `.is_cancelled` + `.on_cancel(fn)`, backed by `threading.Event`; wired from `AgentLoopGraph._cancel_token` into `LLMInvokeOptions.cancellation_token`. |

title: keepalive subscription cleanup on destroy
body: | **keepalive subscription cleanup on destroy** | `ChatStreamGraph`, `ToolRegistryGraph`, and `system_prompt_builder` create keepalive subscriptions (`n.subscribe(lambda: None)`) that are never cleaned up. **Auto-fixable:** add `destroy()` methods that unsubscribe keepalive sinks to prevent leaks in long-lived processes. |

title: AgentLoopGraph.destroy() cancel running loop
body: | **`AgentLoopGraph.destroy()` does not cancel running loop (resolved — internal abort signal)** | `destroy()` sets an internal cancellation flag (`threading.Event`); the `run()` loop checks it between iterations. No polling — reactive cancellation via event signal. Rejected: (b) reject-only (doesn't stop the LLM call); (c) document-as-limitation (violates `destroy()` safety contract). |

title: chat_stream.clear() + append() race
body: | **`chat_stream.clear()` + `append()` race (resolved — serialize via `batch()`)** | Both `clear()` and `append()` internally use `batch()` so they are atomic within a reactive cycle. Callers who need deterministic ordering across multiple mutations use `batch(lambda: (stream.clear(), stream.append(msg)))`. No new mechanism needed — uses existing protocol. Rejected: (b) arbitrary "clear wins" rule; (c) microtask queue (fights the reactive-not-queued invariant). |

title: CancellationToken protocol
body: **Resolved (2026-03-31, implemented 2026-04-04):** `CancellationToken` protocol and `cancellation_token()` factory implemented in `core/cancellation.py`. Exported from `graphrefly.core`. Backed by `threading.Event` with lock-protected callback list, `is_cancelled` property, `on_cancel(fn)` returning an unsubscribe callable, and `cancel()` method. Wiring into async sources (`from_timer`, `from_awaitable`, `from_async_iter`) deferred — the protocol is ready. `TEARDOWN`-via-unsubscribe remains the primary cancellation path; the token is for external/cooperative cancellation.

title: Dual-bookkeeping of derived deps + connect edges
body: **~~Dual-bookkeeping of derived deps + connect edges (all phases, noted 2026-04-05, resolved 2026-04-05):~~** `Graph.add()` now auto-registers edges from constructor deps in both TS and PY. Eliminates the divergence risk between node-level deps and graph-level edge registry. All graph factories no longer need explicit `connect()` calls for constructor deps.

title: takeUntil DATA-only trigger
body: | **`takeUntil` / `take_until` + notifier `DIRTY` (resolved — DATA-only trigger)** | The notifier must emit **`DATA`** to terminate the primary. `DIRTY` is phase-1 transient signaling; termination is permanent and must only trigger on settled phase-2 data. Aligns with compat adapter rule (ignore DIRTY waves). A notifier that only sends DIRTY+RESOLVED (no payload change) never triggers — by design. |

title: zip + partial queues
body: | **`zip` + partial queues (resolved — drop + document)** | When one inner source completes, buffered values that never formed a full tuple are **dropped**; downstream then completes. This matches RxJS behavior and the zip contract (all slots always filled). Callers who need all values should use `combineLatest` or `merge`. Document in JSDoc/docstrings. |

title: concat + ERROR fail-fast
body: | **`concat` + `ERROR` on the second source before the first completes (resolved — fail-fast short-circuit)** | `ERROR` from **any** source (even buffered/inactive) immediately terminates `concat`. Silent error swallowing is a bug magnet; fail-fast is the safer pre-1.0 default. Callers who need “ignore inactive source errors” can wrap source 2 in `retry` or `catchError`. |

title: race + pre-winner DIRTY
body: | **`race` + pre-winner `DIRTY` (resolved — keep current + document)** | Before the first winning `DATA`, `DIRTY` from multiple sources **may** forward downstream. This is transient and harmless — downstream handles it via normal settlement. A stricter “winner-only” implementation adds complexity for minimal gain. Document the behavior clearly in JSDoc/docstrings. |

title: Whole-repo emit → down rename audit + up/backpressure/message_tier sweep
body: TS: emitWithBatch→downWithBatch, _emitToSinks→_downToSinks, _emitAutoValue→_downAutoValue, _boundEmitToSinks→_boundDownToSinks, _emitSequential→_downSequential, emitLine→flushLine. Batch param emit→sink. up() audit: no asymmetries. messageTier() audit: already clean. NodeActions.emit() kept (different semantics). CQRS CommandActions.emit() kept. Spec updated. PY: matching renames (emit_with_batch→down_with_batch, etc.). EmitStrategy→DownStrategy. _manual_emit_used/_manual_emit kept. Pre-1.0, no backward compat concern.

title: PY ReactiveMapBundle parity — .get(key), .has(key), .size + Versioned removal
body: Level A (2026-04-07): Added .get(key), .has(key), .size to PY ReactiveMapBundle matching TS signatures. PY harness strategy.py updated. Level B (TS+PY, 2026-04-07): Removed Versioned wrapper from all reactive bundle APIs (ReactiveMap, ReactiveLog, ReactiveList, ReactiveIndex). .node/.entries/.items/.ordered now emit unwrapped domain types. Internal version counter drives efficient equality without leaking into composition code (spec §5.12). All downstream consumers updated.

title: batch_id in ObserveEvent timeline fields
body: Added batch_id?: number to ObserveEvent in TS (parity with PY). Increments once per subscribe-callback invocation; all messages in one delivery share the same batch_id. Useful for correlating events without polling. in_batch: boolean unchanged. TS: implemented in _createObserveResult, _createObserveResultForAll, and both fallback paths. PY: already had batch_id on batch_seq counter.

title: START protocol + ROM/RAM refactor (v5 architecture)
body: Full NodeImpl/DynamicNodeImpl refactor on shared NodeBase (TS + PY). START handshake as tier 0; tiers shifted (DIRTY/INVALIDATE→1, PAUSE/RESUME→2, DATA/RESOLVED→3, COMPLETE/ERROR→4, TEARDOWN→5). ROM/RAM cache rule, first-run gate, rewire-buffer, at-most-once _active deactivation guard. TS QA: forwardInner START filter, DynamicNodeImpl.up() guard, startWith simplification, _equals in rewire discrepancy check. PY parity: full port + QA pass (terminal replay revert, adapter test restructure, RAM cache clear, initial status fix). _connected field removed in PY. 1429 TS tests, 1156 PY tests pass.

title: Batch drain queue rename to phase vocabulary
body: pendingPhase2/3 renamed to drainPhase2/3/4. drainPhase4 added as the unified TEARDOWN deferral queue. downWithBatch walker relies on pre-sorted input invariant (from bundle.resolve()) and slices once at phase boundaries without re-sorting. Lives in src/core/batch.ts:9-37. Verified in-place 2026-04-12.

title: _addDep + _execFn finally ordering for autoTrackNode two-phase discovery
body: Restored _addDep post-construction dep addition with _isExecutingFn / _pendingRerun re-entrance guard. Finally block runs _pendingRerun BEFORE _clearWaveFlags so autoTrackNode's discovery-then-real-run pattern works. _setInspectorHook restored per-node for Graph.observe. Sibling fixes: compat-layer n.down vs n.emit bug class closed (jotai/nanostores/zustand now emit via framed pipeline). Flag A dataFrom-based emission suppression explicitly closed (architecturally wrong for two-way bridge). Implemented in src/core/node.ts.

title: Flag A — autoTrackNode dataFrom-based emission suppression
body: Closed as architecturally wrong. A compat-layer two-way bridge requires downstream wave correctness: suppressing emit on no-op waves left downstream dep.dirty records stuck forever, and a sibling-dep advance would hang. HEAD's simple autoTrackNode body (no suppression) is correct and cheap. Revisit only if a future optimization can preserve wave correctness — i.e., still emit RESOLVED on no-op waves.

title: GraphReFlyConfig freeze-on-first-use (§2 open item)
body: GraphReFlyConfig class exposes hook getters (bundle/onMessage/onSubscribe) that flip _frozen=true on first read. Setters and registerMessageType throw after freeze. NodeImpl constructor touches one getter to freeze on first node creation. Registry reads (messageTier/isLocalOnly/isKnownMessageType) are free lookups that do NOT freeze. See src/core/config.ts:159–258.

title: src/core/versioning.ts runtime-agnostic crypto
body: Dropped node:crypto import. randomUuid() uses globalThis.crypto.randomUUID() (Node 14.17+, browsers, Deno, Bun, Cloudflare Workers) with a Math.random fallback for ancient runtimes. defaultHash() uses a vendored sync SHA-256 (sha256Hex, ~80 LOC) that matches crypto.createHash('sha256') byte-for-byte — verified cross-check against Node. crypto.subtle.digest was unsuitable because it's async and defaultHash is sync. Core is now importable from browser/edge runtimes. 28 versioning tests still green.

title: Meta TEARDOWN re-entrancy (§10.2 flag 5)
body: Hoisted meta TEARDOWN fan-out out of _updateState into the top of _emit. _updateState stays a pure state-transition walk; meta children receive [[TEARDOWN]] before the parent's cache/status commit. Preserves spec ordering (meta propagates before deactivation) while eliminating the mid-walk re-entrance window where a meta child's dispatch could observe the parent in half-committed state. Implemented at src/core/node.ts:_emit.

title: INVALIDATE measurement cache flush via fn cleanup
body: reactive-layout and reactive-block-layout patterns relied on the deleted v4 per-node onMessage hook to flush measurement caches on INVALIDATE. Rewrote both as raw node(...) with explicit actions.emit + function-form cleanup. Core's _updateState INVALIDATE branch already fires the cleanup (node.ts:1286–1294), so the pattern picks it up for free. Cleanup also fires before next run AND on deactivation — same shape as useEffect/RxJS. 101 reactive-layout tests pass. The two 'FLAG: v5 behavioral change' test comments have been cleared.

title: A3 — Diamond wide scaling via _dirtyDepCount + _sentinelDepCount
body: Replaces O(deps) every(!d.dirty) + every(latestData!==NO_VALUE) scans in _maybeRunFnOnSettlement with O(1) integer >0 checks. Two fields on NodeImpl. Five centralized dep-state transition helpers update both counters in lockstep with DepRecord.dirty/latestData/terminal flags: _depDirtied, _depSettledAsData, _depSettledAsResolved, _depSettledAsTerminal, _depInvalidated. Bulk init in _activate (to deps.length), bulk reset in _deactivate + resubscribable terminal reset. _addDep bumps both counters on new dep. Expected win: diamond wide (10 intermediates) from 184K ops/sec toward the ~680K flat-diamond ceiling. Implementation: src/core/node.ts. Archived from optimizations.md.

title: B3 — equals subtree skip bench variants + correctness regression tests
body: Added three bench variants in src/__bench__/graphrefly.bench.ts under describe('equals: 5-level linear chain, 50% no-op writes'), describe('equals: leaf fn-run count under 50% no-ops'), describe('equals: diamond with 50% no-op inputs'). Each drives inputs that actually exercise the pre-fn skip path (mid-chain values unchanged) so the leaf fn-run count collapses. Also added 2 correctness tests in src/__tests__/core/protocol.test.ts (B3 describe block): assert exact leaf fn-run counts for a 5-level chain and diamond under 50% no-op inputs. These guard against future optimizations that break cascade subtree pruning — the old baseline's 'equals: with (subtree skip)' showed ~1% win because the bench always incremented (equals always false); the new variants show the real benefit materializing on no-op cascades.

title: C0 — PAUSE/RESUME lock id tracking + bufferAll mode
body: Every [PAUSE, lockId] / [RESUME, lockId] tuple must carry a payload; bare throws from _emit's C0 filter loop. Per-node _pauseLocks: Set<unknown> tracks active locks; _paused = _pauseLocks.size > 0. Multi-pauser correctness: lock A release while B held stays paused — downstream nodes on the propagation path track their own locksets symmetrically. Unknown-lockId RESUME is swallowed for dispose() idempotency. pausable: 'resumeAll' (bufferAll) mode captures tier-3/4 outgoing messages into _pauseBuffer while paused; final lock release drains the buffer via recursive _emit before forwarding RESUME downstream. The recursive drain goes through the normal pipeline (tier sort + synthetic DIRTY + equals substitution) so buffered DATAs whose values match the pre-pause cache collapse to RESOLVED — documented as D2 semantic (producers needing pulse semantics should set equals: () => false). QA 2026-04-13 (P1/P2): clear _pauseLocks + _pauseBuffer in _deactivate and in the resubscribable terminal reset block — previously leaked on teardown and could leave a new subscriber stuck paused across a resubscribable reset. Regression test in protocol.test.ts. 5 unit tests total. Lock-id generation lives in src/extra/backpressure.ts (unchanged); bookkeeping is in src/core/node.ts _emit C0 filter loop.

title: _applyVersioning restored + GraphReFlyConfig.defaultVersioning + defaultHashFn
body: Restored NodeImpl._applyVersioning(level, opts?) as a monotonic retroactive upgrade: throws mid-fn (via _isExecutingFn guard), preserves id + version counter across level upgrades. Added GraphReFlyConfig.defaultVersioning + defaultHashFn; every node inherits at construction unless opts.versioning / opts.versioningHash overrides. Graph.setVersioning(level) now retroactively bumps every registered node via _applyVersioning. Graph.add applies graph-level default to newly-added nodes. QA 2026-04-13 refinements: (D3) level detection moved from 'cid' in current shape discriminator to an explicit _versioningLevel: VersioningLevel | undefined field tracked alongside _versioning, future-proof for v2/v3. (D4) _addDep dedups on depNode reference identity. (D1 doc) v0 → v1 upgrade is a fresh history root — new V1 has prev: null, intentional because v0 had no cid to link to; callers needing unbroken cid chains from birth must attach versioning at construction. 3 new unit tests in versioning.test.ts.

title: QA — _addDep dedup + _activate initial-length capture
body: Two related fixes landed together. (1) _addDep now scans _deps for reference-equal depNode and returns the existing index if found, making repeated adds idempotent. autoTrackNode keeps its depIndexMap as a fast-path lookup (returns data[idx] without calling _addDep at all); this is the safety-net backstop. (2) _activate captures initialLen = this._deps.length before its subscribe loop so that if _addDep fires synchronously during a dep's subscribe callback (e.g., autoTrackNode discovery in _execFn), _activate doesn't also subscribe the newly-added dep — double-subscribe bug. _addDep is now the sole subscribe path for runtime-added deps after _activate completes its initial slice. No test regression but closes a counter-drift path surfaced by the QA review.

title: QA — _hasMeta cache removes Object.keys alloc per _emit
body: _emit previously called Object.keys(this.meta) per emission to check whether to run the meta TEARDOWN fan-out block. The call returned an empty array for nodes without meta (the common case) but the allocation itself was wasted on the hot path. Now cached as readonly _hasMeta: boolean at NodeImpl construction (meta is frozen so this never changes) and the fan-out block short-circuits on !_hasMeta. Minor perf, no correctness impact.

title: Higher-order operators: fn+closure tier-1 upgrade
body: All four `*Map` operators upgraded to fn+closure B pattern. Key decisions: `{ deactivation }` cleanup (not `() => void` — prevents inner teardown before terminal waves); `exhaustMap` emits RESOLVED on drop; `mergeMap` switched from inline `spawn()` to `forwardInner` (fixes inner ERROR leak + START forwarding). Wave-batching claim was revised: fn+closure doesn't auto-coalesce multiple outer DATAs. See `SESSION-extras-wave1-audit.md`. **QA pass (2026-04-13) fixed:** (a) `mergeMap` `spawn()` TDZ crash when inner completes synchronously (`const stop` → `let stop`); (b) all `*Map` deactivation now resets `sourceDone = false` (was stale across reactivation cycles, caused premature COMPLETE on re-subscribe); (c) `_updateState` `newCache` deferred write was overwriting INVALIDATE/TEARDOWN cache clears — mirrored those clears to `newCache`.

title: `switchMap` batch loop intermediate subscribe churn
body: Short-circuited to `batch0[batch0.length - 1]`; `clearInner()` called once; N-1 intermediate `project()` calls eliminated.

title: `forwardInner` forwards PAUSE/RESUME/TEARDOWN from inner to outer sinks
body: `else` branch narrowed to only forward DIRTY/RESOLVED/INVALIDATE. PAUSE, RESUME, TEARDOWN from inner dropped — inner flow-control and lifecycle signals are internal to the `*Map` operator. RxJS/callbag precedent: no backpressure forwarding in merge-style operators. For mergeMap, one inner's PAUSE must not pause all output.
